1998-04-01.title: Greek Orthodox Church Icons Ravaged in the Turkish Part of Cyprus
1998-04-01.abstract: Looting of Greek Orthodox churches in northern Cyprus, where Turkish troops landed in 1974, has brought hundreds of magnificant artworks onto international art market and, in recent months, led to series of spectacular raids by police in Germany; Bavarian police recovered collection of stolen Greek Orthodox icons in Oct and arrested Aydin Dikman, Turkish citizen who has lived in Germany since 1961; German authorities say he is one of Europe's most prolific art thieves; map; photos (M)

1998-04-01.title: On the Road to Talks: Israeli Pace Annoys U.S.
1998-04-01.abstract: Steven Erlanger analysis notes that US negotiator Dennis B Ross made some headway during his recent meetings with Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, but Sec of State Madeleine K Albright said that it was 'not nearly enough' for breakthrough in impasse; contends that stalemate is not only damaging Israel's position in Middle East but Washington's too; photo (M)

1998-04-01.title: Botswana Is Pleased To Be Host To Clinton
1998-04-01.abstract: Pres Clinton visits Mokolodi Nature Preserve in Botswana and goes on safari with Hillary Rodham Clinton in Chobe River Valley; Clinton, in speech at nature preserve, calls for Senate approval of environmental treaty to limit growth of deserts; map; photo (M)

1998-04-01.title: Albania Calls In European Monitors to Watch Kosovo Border
1998-04-01.abstract: Florian Raunig, Albanian-speaking Austrian, is head of four-member civilian monitoring group that has been posted by Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe to assess conditions in lawless territory that runs along Albanian-Serbian border (M)

1998-04-01.title: Hong Kong Journal; A Bit of the Old Empire Shows Britons the Door
1998-04-01.abstract: British residents in Hong Kong were granted one-year extension of their visas in April 1997, four months before Britain handed former Crown Colony back to China; those visas expire en masse on March 31, and thousands of Britons must prepare to leave; British leaving this time are generally young; they work as bartenders, waitresses and short-order cooks in pubs and restaurants; photo (M)

1998-04-02.title: Inquiry to Ask if Nazi Loot May Also Be in U.S.
1998-04-02.abstract: Clinton Administration and Sen Alfonse M D'Amato, who has led investigations into wealth looted by Nazi Germany, say they will set up commission to determine if any Holocaust-era assets are still in American banks (M)

1998-04-02.title: In a Process That's 'Going to Be Years': World Bank's Estimate For Jakarta:$3 Billion
1998-04-02.abstract: SINGAPORE—  The World Bank is warning that $3 billion in emergency food and medical aid will be needed over the next year to help Indonesia provide essential supplies to millions of people affected by drought and the economic crisis.

1998-04-02.title: Joining Euro A Dim Hope For Greece
1998-04-02.abstract: Greece, which did not make list of 11 countries ready to adopt euro in 1999, now ponders its chances for becoming eligible in 2001 despite its belated efforts to trim its debt and shrink its overindulged state sector; joining the euro club is goal firmly held by Prime Min Costas Simitis and cherished by many Greeks (M)

1998-04-02.title: U.S. Reports China Has Agreed to Free Tiananmen Leader
1998-04-02.abstract: China agrees to release a noted dissident, Wang Dan, for medical reasons and send him into exile; release is part of agreement by US to drop sponsorship of resolution condemning China's human rights policies; Wang has been at top of list of Chinese dissidents that Clinton Administration has been trying to get released (M)

1998-04-02.title: U.N. Derides Scientologists' Charges About German 'Persecution'
1998-04-02.abstract: United Nations special investigator Abdelfattah Amor rejects charges by Scientology movement that German Government is using Nazi tactics of persecution and ill treatment against it (M)

1998-04-03.title: 4 Salvadorans Say They Killed U.S. Nuns on Orders of Military
1998-04-03.abstract: Four former Salvadoran national guardsmen convicted of killing three American nuns and a lay worker in 1980 say for first time that they acted only after receiving 'orders from above'; El Salvador and US have always officially argued that killers acted on their own; human rights groups and relatives of victims--Maura Clark, Jean Donovan, Ita Ford and Dorothy Kazel--have always maintained that executions were ordered by military authorities; United Nations Truth Commission report in 1993 concluded that Col Carlos Eugenio Vides Casanova and Gen Jose Guillermo Garcia had ordered official coverup; both men have been granted residence in US and now live in Florida; four guardsmen are Daniel Canales Ramirez, Carlos Joaquin Contreras Palacios, Francisco Orlando Contreras Recinos and Jose Roberto Moreno Canjura; photos (M)

1998-04-03.title: Indonesia to Sign 3d I.M.F. Rescue Pact
1998-04-03.abstract: Indonesia is within days of signing new accord with International Monetary Fund to try to rescue its collapsing economy, and fund's leadership is fending off criticism that it is giving in too much to demands of Pres Suharto; new accord delays imposition of earlier requirements that Indonesia end its subsidies on electricity, gasoline and number of basic food items; Indonesian officials say fund's attempt to terminate subsidies are stoking anti-Government protests across country (M)

1998-04-03.title: Israelis Accused in Killing
1998-04-03.abstract: Hamas and Palestinian officials accuse Israel of assassinating a suspected Hamas bomb maker, Muhyiaddin al-Sharif, on West Bank (S)

1998-04-03.title: Ex-Vichy Aide Is Convicted And Reaction Ranges Wide
1998-04-03.abstract: French court finds Maurice Papon guilty of complicity in Nazi crimes against humanity because as a Vichy official he turned Jews over to the Germans in World War II; imposes 10-year prison sentence, with deprivation of civil rights; finding both comforts and disconcerts country Papon served as high-ranking civil servant for 50 years; Papon remains free while he appeals, and both sentence and his continuing freedom draw criticism from those who expected more severe sentence; photo (M)

1998-04-03.title: The Clintons as Teammates, Dovetailing in Africa
1998-04-03.abstract: Hillary Rodham Clinton proves a political asset to Pres Clinton during their trip to Africa, which she visited a year ago and pressed her husband to visit; White House aides acknowledge that Clinton partnership has been more visible in Africa than it has been since 1994; map; photo (M)

1998-04-04.title: Ex-Vichy Aide Is Ordered to Pay $750,000 in Damages and Fees
1998-04-04.abstract: Maurice Papon, who was convicted in France of having been accomplice to Nazi crimes against humanity during World War II, is ordered by French criminal court to pay nearly $750,000 in damages and legal fees to victims and their lawyers (M)

1998-04-04.title: Bangkok Journal; Was That a Lady I Saw You Boxing?
1998-04-04.abstract: Interview with 16-year-old Parinya Kiatbusaba, Thailand's kick-boxing sensation, who is also transvestite; photos (M)

1998-04-04.title: Companies Are Investigated For Aid to China on Rockets
1998-04-04.abstract: Federal grand jury is investigating whether Loral Space and Communications and Hughes Electronics illegally gave China space expertise that significantly advanced Beijing's ballistic missile program; officials say criminal inquiry was dealt serious blow when Pres Clinton recently approved export to China of similar technology from Loral; decision was opposed by Justice Department, which argued that it would be difficult to prosecute companies if Government gave blessings to deal; companies have denied wrongdoing (M)

1998-04-04.title: Clinton Warns Japan: Fire Up Economy to Stem a Decline
1998-04-04.abstract: Pres Clinton warns Japan in unusually explicit terms that it must change course if it hopes to pull economy out of long-running decline that has worsened in recent weeks; says country's current strategy is clearly not appropriate to times; praises Prime Min Ryutaro Hashimoto as able man, but says there is clearly ongoing struggle with entrenched bureaucracy; comments add to pressure from Treas Sec Robert E Rubin and leaders of several of world's biggest economic powers, who have repeatedly warned that Japan's economic decline and its inability to absorb imports from neighboring countries threaten to hurt Asia's fragile recovery; yen falls to six-and-a-half-year low against dollar in mounting evidence that Japan is heading into one of worst downturns since end of World War II (M)

1998-04-04.title: Unemployment Goes Up but Optimism on Economy Prevails : U.S. Stocks Breach the 9,000 Barrier
1998-04-04.abstract: NEW YORK—  The Dow Jones industrial average topped 9,000 points for the first time Friday, reflecting seemingly insatiable demand for stocks from investors around the world.

1998-04-05.title: For Greens Of Germany, The Outlook Is Murky
1998-04-05.abstract: Germany's opposition Greens party, which seemed to be staking out role as political kingmaker not too long ago, is being depicted more as court jester; swing might be easily dismissed were it not for fact that come Sept, German voters will decide who will lead nation into new millennium; because of Germany's political system, small parties like ecologically minded Greens are coalition-builders that help carry big parties to power (M)

1998-04-05.title: West Hinders Inquiry on Dumping as Rights Issue
1998-04-05.abstract: United Nations Commission on Human Rights investigator Fatma-Zohra Ksentini report names US, Germany, Australia, Britain and Netherlands as biggest toxic-waste exporters, and says masses of toxic waste are still being sent to Africa; Ksentini says dumping is human rights violation (M)

1998-04-05.title: In Mexico and the U.S., New Visas Produce Stress
1998-04-05.abstract: US authorities, in sweeping new effort to control travel across Mexican border, have begun calling in border-crossing documents held for decades by millions of Mexicans to replace them with high-tech visas carrying digital fingerprint; documents being replaced allow over 100 million legal border crossings each year by business people, tourists and shoppers from Mexico; hope is that new cards, which State Dept calls laser visas, will help screen out criminals and illegal immigrants; replacement of old border-crossing cards has many Mexicans upset by $45 processing fee and nervous that their applications may be denied; map; photo (M)

1998-04-05.title: Case Study Shows Why Bosnians Shun Banks
1998-04-05.abstract: Officers of SAB bank in Sarajevo, Bosnia, say large cash accounts of embassies, World Bank, European Commission and other international organizations are frozen and perhaps lost; dispute over bank's ownership caused a run, and depositors can no longer withdraw their money; some foreign banking experts say they expect trouble to be cleared up soon, but many Bosnians say they believe foreigners do not know Bosnia (M)

1998-04-05.title: Sudan Christians Take War's Culture Clash North
1998-04-05.abstract: Sudan is ruled by conservative Islamic party that has sought to spread Islam among people and discourage other religions; Government has fought to subdue southern Christian rebels who want return to secular government; despite this, Khartoum's churches are filled to overflowing with Christians, worshipping freely, and those congregations are growing; Government has refused to permit any new churches to be built in city or outlying slums, and it has bulldozed dozens of Christian schools; map; photo (M)

1998-04-06.title: Ranks of Albanian Rebels Increase in Kosovo
1998-04-06.abstract: Albanian rebel groups in Kosovo, Serbia, have swelled in numbers in recent weeks--in part by infusion of new troops and weapons smuggled over border from Albania; there are indications guerrillas have been joined by mercenaries; rapid and startling growth of insurgency has prompted fears that unrest in province could explode into next Balkan war; map (M)

1998-04-06.title: ISRAEL AT 50: Many Voices, Not All in Unison, in the Spirited Israel of the 1990's; THE SECULAR JEWS: Beauty Is Truth. That Is All the Stylish Need to Know.
1998-04-06.abstract: Series, Israel at 50, on ethnic and cultural diversity that has evolved in half-century since founding as Jewish state; interview with Ilan and Aviva Pivko, affluent secular Jews, who defend their hedonism as necessary embellishment that creates environment that looks and feels better, and who regard Israeli-Arab conflict and violence it has spawned as backgound noise; photo (M)

1998-04-06.title: ISRAEL AT 50: Many Voices, Not All in Unison, in the Spirited Israel of the 1990's; THE SEPHARDIM: A Sense of Grievance Over a Life Spent 'on Standby'
1998-04-06.abstract: Series, Israel at 50, on ethnic and cultural diversity that has evolved in half-century since founding as Jewish state; Sephardic Jews, immigrants or descendants of immigrants from North Africa and Middle East who make up 48 percent of Israel's population, carry keen sense of grievance for what they consider discrimination because of Sephardic background; level of their schooling and unemployment rate has been below that of European, or Ashkenazic Jews; residents of Netivot Sephardic community comment; photo (M)

1998-04-06.title: ISRAEL AT 50: Many Voices, Not All in Unison, in the Spirited Israel of the 1990's; THE ISRAELI ARABS: Too Israeli an Arab, and Vice Versa
1998-04-06.abstract: Series, Israel at 50, on ethnic and cultural diversity that has evolved in half-century since founding as Jewish state; fate has left many Israeli Arabs between two worlds--in which they are second-class citizens among Israelis and Israelis among Arabs; actress Salwa Nakkara, Arab born and raised in Israel, comments (M)

1998-04-06.title: Edgy Strangers Happily Resist The Melting Pot
1998-04-06.abstract: Series, Israel at 50, on ethnic and cultural diversity that has evolved in half-century since founding as Jewish state; roughly 800,000 immigrants who swept into country with weakening and collapse of Soviet state have carved out special niche while happily resisting melting pot; some residents of Russian community in Ashdod say they are in harmony in Israel, even though they regard non-Russian Israelis as 'others;' Bella Portnova, violinist with Ashdod chamber orchestra, comments; photo (M)

1998-04-07.title: Ulster Talks Get Urgent Compromise Plan
1998-04-07.abstract: Former US Senator George J Mitchell, mediator at Northern Ireland peace talks, presents Protestant and Roman Catholic negotiators with set of urgent compromise proposals as deadline nears for peace settlement; says proposals will give negotiators comprehensive look at many problems that persist, but that both sides are close to bringing talks to successful conclusion; photos (M)

1998-04-07.title: South African Military Chief Quits Over False Coup Report
1998-04-07.abstract: Gen Georg Meiring, chief of South Africa's Army criticized for role in presenting President Nelson Mandela with bogus report on left-wing plot to overthrow Government, resigns; says he has lost trust he needs to function properly; has been widely criticized for handling of report, which never went through proper military intelligence channels and was reportedly based on single source; photo (M)

1998-04-07.title: Hamas Killed Its Own, Say Palestinians
1998-04-07.abstract: Palestinian Authority says Muhyiaddin al-Sharif, suspected bomb-maker found slain in West Bank and who was wanted by Israel for series of suicide bombings that killed scores of Israelis, was murdered in fued within militant Islamic group Hamas, and not by Israel; arrests five members of group for murder; Hamas dismisses findings and renews threat to carry out revenge attacks against Israel, which has emphatically denied any role in killing (M)

1998-04-07.title: 2 Brazil Towns Fight for Road Closed for a Wildlife Park
1998-04-07.abstract: Brazilian conservationists are locked in dispute with residents of area near Iguacu Falls National Park over permanent closing of 13-mile road that runs through park, which is one of Brazil's most popular tourist attractions and has been designated by UNESCO as part of world's patrimony; residents say closing brought them severe economic hardships (M)

1998-04-07.title: Iran and Iraq Begin Big Trade of P.O.W.'s
1998-04-07.abstract: Iran and Iraq, in important step toward resolving their bitter enmity, begin major exchange of prisoners of war held since end of long and bloody war decade ago; approximately 4,000 prisoners, majority Iraqis, have been repatriated; vast majority of those still in captivity are held by Iran (M)

1998-04-08.title: Q & A / J. Brian Atwood, U.S. Development Agency : Clinton Seeks Better Start in Africa, and France Understands Why
1998-04-08.abstract: Fresh from accompanying President Bill Clinton on his trip to Africa, J. Brian Atwood, head of the U.S. Agency for International Development, held talks with international and French government officials in Paris. He also spoke with IHT editors and reporters, and following are excerpts of his remarks about U.S. assistance to African countries.

1998-04-08.title: Paris Journal; If You Hear Mozart Arias, This Is a Metro Station
1998-04-08.abstract: Paris Journal on new regulations for musicians who want to perform in Metro: they may do so in station corridors, but not on platforms or in cars, and must audition before Metro official to obtain badges; Antoine Naso, who runs badge program, says he tries to imagine what public would like to hear; photo of American singer-guitarist Morty Baumann auditioning; map; rules have led to decline in number of performers, and marked improvement in quality of music heard (M)

1998-04-08.title: Yeltsin Tries to Garner Legislators' Votes for His Premier Nominee
1998-04-08.abstract: Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin summons opponents to Kremlin for moment of political theater, to demonstrate his grip on power and show support for his new Prime Minister, Sergei N Kiriyenko; television cameras capture Yeltsin projecting image of strength, although meeting itself is somewhat less than definitive (S)

1998-04-08.title: New U.S. Diplomat Tries to Speak Japan's Language
1998-04-08.abstract: Thomas S Foley, former US House Speaker who is now Ambassador to Japan, is employing soft, diplomatic approach, and trying to learn Japanese, at time when US is using blunt language to get Japan to stimulate its economy; Foley argues same case, but with less heat; he also enjoys luxurious mansion, large staff and status as minor deity in Tokyo; photo (M)

1998-04-08.title: The Chinese Burst Onto the Tourist Scene
1998-04-08.abstract: Chinese tourists are now flocking overseas, spending handfuls of cash and emerging as major source of tourism income for battered economies of Southeast Asia; planeloads of Chinese arrive daily in Thailand, most popular and affordable destination; photos; Chinese tourists now roam and shop freely, without Chinese officials who used to keep close watch; Shanghai travel agent says he hopes to take groups to Hawaii, but US is not yet issuing routine tourist visas (M)

1998-04-09.title: Cairo Journal; Blazing a Fresh Trail, Getting a Rancid Welcome
1998-04-09.abstract: Daniel Kurtzer, new US Ambassador to Egypt, is Orthodox Jew and first Jew to be US Ambassador to major Arab country; that has stirred some mistrust and resentment in Cairo; Osama al-Baz, top adviser to Pres Hosni Mubarak, has criticized Egyptian journalists for their hostile reception of Kurtzer, whom he portrays as true friend of Arabs; photos (M)

1998-04-09.title: With a Deadline Tonight, Ulster Negotiators Study Compromises
1998-04-09.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair and Irish Prime Min Bertie Ahern are trying to work out compromises on disputed issues between Protestant and Roman Catholic political leaders in effort to meet April 9 deadline for peace agreement in Northern Ireland (M)

1998-04-09.title: Are 'Smart' Cards Getting Too Wise to Get Off the Drawing Board?
1998-04-09.abstract: PARIS—  Almost no one disputes the potential of "smart" cards. The ubiquitous plastic cards embedded with foil-covered computer chips are being used for everything from pay phones to parking meters, from vending machines to mobile phones.

1998-04-09.title: Q & A / Edmund Jensen, President of Visa : Large Retailers May Spur Revolution in Chip Cards
1998-04-09.abstract: Edmund Jensen, president and chief executive of Visa International Inc., is retiring in December after a four-year tenure that saw Visa expand into chip-card technology. He recently discussed the future of smart cards with Gina Rarick of the International Herald Tribune.

1998-04-09.title: Morocco Finds Fundamentalism Benign but Scary
1998-04-09.abstract: Militant Islamic groups have been mobilizing and expanding in Morocco; they have gained followers in shantytowns in Casablanca and other cities by assisting sick and unemployed, and are recruiting high school teachers and university students; photo (M)

1998-04-10.title: Experts Dispute Iraq On Germ Weapons
1998-04-10.abstract: Independent experts who reviewed Iraq's progress in eliminating biological weapons at Baghdad's request have rejected President Saddam Hussein's contention that he no longer has a germ warfare program.    Their report called Iraqi disclosures ''incomplete and inadequate,'' and said Baghdad had failed to convince them that biological weapons have been eliminated.

1998-04-10.title: Rome Journal; Pope's Little Army Scrambles to Recruit a Chief
1998-04-10.abstract: Swiss Guards, colorfully dressed soldiers who have defended Pope and Vatican gates since 16th century, are in want of leader; search for suitable commander, which has dragged on for five months, has become baffling mystery inside Vatican and beyond; honor, once coveted by Swiss aristocrats, has lost some of its luster; to noblemen and commoners alike, salary is uninviting, reportedly about $30,000 a year; Lt Col Alois Estermann is favorite to be appointed commander, but he is not aristocrat; his photo (M)

1998-04-10.title: Britain and the Irish, North and South, Work Toward Ulster Accord
1998-04-10.abstract: Negotiators in Northern Ireland peace talks miss their April 10 deadline for settlement but work feverishly into morning hours; negotiations are being guided by Prime Ministers Tony Blair of Britain and Bertie Ahern of Ireland as representatives of eight participating Northern Ireland political parties work to reach consensus on final draft; draft was presented to them on April 7 by George J Mitchell, former US Senate majority leader who is chairman of talks; photo (M)

1998-04-10.title: Romanian Confesses to Role In a Religious Artifact Ring
1998-04-10.abstract: NEWARK, New Jersey—  A Romanian citizen living in the United States admitted in court this week that he was part of a ring that had stolen religious artifacts from France and that he was trying to sell them in the United States, federal officials said.

1998-04-10.title: U.N., Stymied by Congo Leader, Halts Inquiry on Rwanda Killings
1998-04-10.abstract: United Nations suspends its investigation into allegations of massacres of Rwandan refugees in Congo, after weeks of harassment and detention of Canadian member of team, Christopher Harland; United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights Mary Robinson says there is no longer any excuse for behavior of Pres Laurent Kabila's Government in Congo (M)

1998-04-11.title: Palestinians and Hamas Feud Over Killing of a Bomb Maker
1998-04-11.abstract: Palestinian Authority and Hamas meet to defuse growing feud over who killed Muhyiaddin al-Sharif, Hamas bomb-maker wanted by Israel for several terrorist attacks who was found murdered in Ramallah; Palestinian Authority accuses expatriate leaders of Hamas of trying to provoke confrontation by pushing internal leaders to break longstanding agreement not to criticize Palestinian leadership in public; accuses Hamas of killing Sharif in internal feud out of suspicion he was collaborating with Israel (M)

1998-04-11.title: U.S. Says North Korea Helped Develop New Pakistani Missile
1998-04-11.abstract: US officials and private analysts say Pakistan's test of new liquid-fueled missile able to strike deep inside India is based on technology smuggled from North Korea; say weapon is enhanced version of North Korean Rodong missile, which is advanced version of Scud missile developed in Moscow in 1960's (M)

1998-04-11.title: Bogota Journal; Emeralds' Luster Hides a Darker Side
1998-04-11.abstract: Article examines Colombia's emerald trade, which has been enmeshed in murky undercurrent of drug trafficking and violence involving Medellin drug lords wishing to expand money laundering; trade is controlled by Victor Carranza, who is under arrest for raising paramilitary force of 2,000 men that reportedly operated as death squad; US intelligence officials say he is major drug trafficker and smuggler; Colombia produces 60 percent of world's emeralds; map; photos (M)

1998-04-11.title: On Brink of Peace, Catholics and Protestants Pray
1998-04-11.abstract: Catholics and Protestants in Belfast react positively to tentative peace accord; many gather together to pray; photo (M)

1998-04-11.title: Mitchell Feared the Killing of a Leader
1998-04-11.abstract: Interview with former US Sen George J Mitchell, Northern Ireland peace negotiator, who says he accelerated pace of negotiations that led to agreement after threat by unidentified paramilitary group to murder high-profile person associated with talks (M)

1998-04-12.title: Yeltsin's Nominee, Besieged, Is Not Without Allies
1998-04-12.abstract: 35-year-old Sergei Kiriyenko, Pres Boris Yeltsin's besieged nominee for Prime Minister, has shown himself to be adept politician in brief period on national stage; his liabilities, mainly his youth and limited Government experience, are also his potential strengths since he has not been in Government long enough to make many enemies; he may be leader who can pursue market-economy measures without debilitating ideological battles that hobbled some predecessors; photo (M)

1998-04-12.title: Famous Name in Fellowships Turns to Art Exhibits
1998-04-12.abstract: Interview with Harriet Mayor Fulbright, wife of former US Sen J William Fulbright, who is visiting Paris to discuss progress on exchange of French and American exhibits of popular art on behalf of President's Committee on the Arts and Humanities (M)

1998-04-12.title: Aid Groups Are Hands That Help In Bosnia
1998-04-12.abstract: Three years after Dayton peace accords committed NATO countries and international organizations to rebuild shattered economy of Bosnia, $7 billion spent annually is paying off as reconstruction of country is picking up speed; largely responsible for efforts are nearly 200 nongovernmental organizations from 24 countries that will spend approximately $1 billion in 1998 rebuilding infrastructure and social services (M)

1998-04-12.title: Palestinian Police Arrest Suspected Hamas Killer
1998-04-12.abstract: Palestinian police arrest dozens of Hamas members in West Bank and Gaza Strip, including alleged leader of plot to kill another member of militant Islamic movement; Hamas dismisses allegations as 'mockery' and issues statement that its own investigation confirmed that Palestinian Authority chief, in collusion with Israel, killed Muhyiaddin al-Sharif, member of organization's military wing held responsible by Israel for several terror attacks (M)

1998-04-12.title: 11 Foreigners Are Detained In Mexico Raid
1998-04-12.abstract: Mexican police raid farming cooperative in Chiapas state after Indian supporters of Zapatista rebels declared independence from state government; arrest 20 people, including 11 foreigners, some of whom were human rights observers (S)

1998-04-13.title: In New York, as in Belfast, A Blend of Irish Voices
1998-04-13.abstract: Many of loudest voices among Irish New Yorkers react with suspicion or derision to agreement intended to end strife between Catholics and Protestants in Northern Ireland; but in Irish neighborhoods of city, many Irish-Americans gradually begin to accept agreement as stunning but welcome gift (M)

1998-04-13.title: Pope's Words On Easter Call For Dialogue Toward Peace
1998-04-13.abstract: Pope John Paul II delivers grave and at times dark Easter message at St Peter's Square; warns of fratricidal strife and slaughter that still exists around world; photo (M)

1998-04-13.title: U.S. BUSINESS ROLE IN POLICY ON CHINA IS UNDER QUESTION
1998-04-13.abstract: Clinton Administration handling of satellite technology exports to China by key Clinton supporters illustrates competing forces that buffet President on China policy; classified Pentagon report in May 1997 concluded that scientists from Hughes Electronics and Loral Space and Communications had turned over expertise that significantly improved reliability of China's nuclear missiles, even though companies were ordered to keep tight rein on such sophisticated technology sought by Chinese military; criminal probe of companies was reportedly undermined when Clinton approved Loral's exports to China; White House denied any political interference in issue, but Chinese reportedly concluded that Administration policy on issues like spread of weapons and human rights abuses could be reversed by corporations; Loral is headed by largest personal donor to Democratic Party last year; Administration's China policy has come under intense Congressional scrutiny in last year; at issue is influence American contributors have on China policy (M)

1998-04-13.title: For Most in Belfast, an Easter of Joy and Hope
1998-04-13.abstract: People of Northern Ireland go to their churches to celebrate Easter and to pray for permanent peace promised by historic agreement signed by leaders of Protestant majority and Roman Catholic minority; many of them barely remember when there was no sectarian violence; forgiveness and hope for peace are themes at most Catholic and Protestant churches, but not at breakaway church founded by Rev Ian Paisley, who kept his Democratic Unionist Party out of peace negotiations; Paisley, publicly described by opponents as xenophobe and fascist, attacks peace agreement and urges his congregation of 300 mostly older people to vote against it in May 22 referendum; one of his congregants voices fear about terrorists being released, theme Paisley has been stressing; under peace accord, many Catholic and Protestant paramilitary prisoners would be eligible for early release within two years; photo (M)

1998-04-13.title: Despite Gains, North Korea Is Pressed On Food Aid
1998-04-13.abstract: Catherine Bertini, executive director of United Nations World Food Program, says young children in North Korea appear better nourished than they did year ago, but serious food shortage continues there; says North Korean officials are still not cooperating fully with her agency, which solicits and distributes most of aid to nation of 23 million people; several reports suggest that food aid earmarked for general public is being eaten or stockpiled by officials and army; reports of groups such as Doctors Without Borders are far more dire than Bertini's assessment (M)

1998-04-14.title: Heavy Hand Squelches Yeltsin Foe
1998-04-14.abstract: Human rights activists, Russian press and some of Moscow's leading politicians accuse Government of Pres Boris Yeltsin of engaging in clumsy power play by annulling victory of populist Andrei Klimentyev, who won race for Mayor of Nizhny Novogorod in election that was embarrassment for advocates of market reform; Klimentyev has since been dragged to jail to face corruption charges; town is highly touted bastion of reform and hometown of Sergei Kiriyenko, designated Prime Minister, and of Boris Nemtsov, top Kremlin aide; map; photo (M)

1998-04-14.title: On Far Right, A Soft Voice Undermines French Center
1998-04-14.abstract: Interview with Bruno Megret, soft voice of France's far right who is regarded as brain behind political strategy that has convulsed French politics by undermining country's political center (M)

1998-04-14.title: Rome Journal; A Delphic Oracle Has Seen the Future, and Likes It
1998-04-14.abstract: Interview with Italian business tycoon Giovanni Agnelli, who says he welcomes change to single European currency and to more open markets; map; photo (M)

1998-04-14.title: Apartheid-Era Chief Said to Yield to Panel
1998-04-14.abstract: Former South African Pres P W Botha reportedly agrees to appear before country's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, day before he was to stand trial for refusing to obey repeated commission subpoenas (S)

1998-04-14.title: Nations Bank Drives $62 Billion Merger : A New BankAmerica:Biggest of U.S. Banks
1998-04-14.abstract: NEW YORK—  Having decided that there is safety in big numbers, two pairs of American banks announced $90 billion worth of mergers Monday, bulking up to compete more efficiently in an international industry that increasingly emphasizes size.

1998-04-15.title: Turkish Commandos Capture a Kurdish Leader in Raid Into Iraq
1998-04-15.abstract: Turkish commandos stage raid into northern Iraq and capture Kurdish rebel commander, Semdin Sakik, who recently defected from guerrilla leadership (S)

1998-04-15.title: As First Step in Peace Accord, Ireland Frees 9 I.R.A. Prisoners
1998-04-15.abstract: Nine IRA members are released from Irish prison early to strengthen support for Northern Ireland peace agreement; move is considered show of support for Sinn Fein leader Gerry Adams, who faces internal opposition to accord (S)

1998-04-15.title: Looking for Compromise, South Africa Postpones Botha Trial
1998-04-15.abstract: Trial of former South African Pres P W Botha is postponed for day while his lawyers and Truth and Reconciliation Commission negotiate to avoid embarrassing and politically divisive proceedings; 82-year-old Botha faces contempt charges for ignoring subpoenas to testify about anti-apartheid policies (S)

1998-04-15.title: U.S. Treasury Chief Offers Plan to Avoid Crises Like Asia's
1998-04-15.abstract: Treasury Sec Robert Rubin outlines US initiative to avoid future economic convulsions in Asia; calls for drastic updating of international financial markets, and requirement that nations disclose embarrassing financial data; he and Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan propose that US consider barring foreign banks from operating in US unless home countries impose strong regulation on their financial institutions; Indonesia, Thailand and South Korea all lacked such controls before their banks began crumbling under weight of bad debt and plunging currencies (M)

1998-04-15.title: Teheran Mayor's Arrest Focuses Iranian Power Struggle
1998-04-15.abstract: Arrest of Teheran's mayor, Gholamhossein Karabaschi, becomes focus for struggle between hard-liners and moderates since election of Pres Mohammad Khatami; Khatami supporters hope for greater political and cultural tolerance and protest mayor's arrest; photo; conservatives denounce Karabaschi as corrupt and hail arrest as part of anti-corruption campaign (S)

1998-04-16.title: U.S. Is Trying to Defuse a Growing Russia-Latvia Confrontation
1998-04-16.abstract: United States works quietly with other nations to defuse growing confrontation between Russia and Latvia that theatens to damage Washington's already fraying relationship with Moscow; Latvian Cabinet has agreed on changes in its citizenship laws to answer Russian contention that Latvia mistreats Russians who live there; American official says Clinton Administration is concerned over Moscow's 'bullying rhetoric and tactics' against Latvia, including threats of economic sanctions (M)

1998-04-16.title: Ancient Hindu Festival Thrives in Computer-Age India
1998-04-16.abstract: Throngs of Hindus bath in Ganges during kumbh mela, major festival at Hardwar, in northern India; millions of pilgrims gather at holy site, one of four where it is celebrated; photo; map (M)

1998-04-16.title: Botha Trial Begins After Talks to Avoid It Fail
1998-04-16.abstract: Trial of former South African Pres P W Botha begins after negotiations to avoid proceedings against him break down; Botha, defiant apartheid-era leader, is known for his stubborn and combative way when he led country; he faces charges of contempt for refusing to go before country's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, which wants to question him about his role in authorizing widespread artrocities that took place during his decade in power; Truth Commission, hoping to avoid politically divisive spectacle of taking Botha to court, had agreed to many concessions Botha had sought, but in the end it was not enough (M)

1998-04-16.title: Easing Its Stand on Detentions, Israel Frees a Palestinian
1998-04-16.abstract: Israel frees longest-serving Palestinian prisoner held without trial, signaling softening of policy criticized as one of country's most serious violations of human rights; Ahmad Qatamesh had been jailed for 5 years and 7 months without trial on suspicion of being leader of Popular Front for Liberation of Palestine, militant group opposed to Israeli-Palestinian accords; his release follows growing campaign by Israeli human rights groups, lawyers, leftist politicians and writers against detention without trial; Qatamesh denies Israeli television report that he agreed to renounce anti-Israeli violence in exchange for his release; photo (M)

1998-04-16.title: Amid Uncertainty, Policymakers Talk Up Currency : Trans-Atlantic Cheer for Euro
1998-04-16.abstract: WASHINGTON—  The European commissioner overseeing the historic shift to a single currency brought a message of reassurance here Wednesday, saying that while the advent of the euro will represent "an unprecedented challenge," the transition from 11 currencies to one should bolster the global economy without unduly roiling foreign exchange markets.

1998-04-17.title: German Ornes, 78, Publisher And Advocate of Free Press
1998-04-17.abstract: German Emilio Ornes, influential publisher of newspaper El Caribe in Dominican Republic and longtime champion of freedom of the press in Latin America, dies at age 78; photo (M)

1998-04-17.title: U.N. REPORT SEES NO IRAQI PROGRESS ON WEAPONS ISSUE
1998-04-17.abstract: Report by United Nations chief arms inspector, Richard Butler, concludes that Iraq is no closer to meeting requirements for lifting of sanctions than it was last fall, when Baghdad began to disrupt efforts to locate and destroy its remaining weapons of mass destruction; evidence in report of Iraq's failure to provide any new information on its weapons, coupled with new outburst of defiance from Baghdad, raises once again prospect of confrontation between Iraq and United States, which has twice threatened military action against Iraq for impeding weapons inspections; Butler's report is turned over to Sec Gen Kofi Annan and is likely to go to Security Council by weekend; Iraqis step up pressure to have sanctions lifted, a move diplomats did not expect in earnest until next sanctions review in October (M)

1998-04-17.title: Japan's Lack of Leadership Pushes ASEAN Toward Cooperation With China
1998-04-17.abstract: SINGAPORE—  China has used its strong economy and apparent immunity to Asia's financial turmoil to gain significant diplomatic favor with its previously suspicious neighbors at the expense of Japan, which has failed to meet regional expectations of leadership, officials and analysts say.

1998-04-17.title: Survivor of Killing Fields Is Resolute in Quest for Justice
1998-04-17.abstract: Dith Pran, Cambodian journalist whose ordeal of forced labor, starvation and beatings by Khmer Rouge was portrayed in 1984 film The Killing Fields, says that Pol Pot's death may have dashed hopes to try him for crimes against humanity, but adds that it will not end quest for justice by survivors of his four-year reign of terror (M)

1998-04-17.title: Apple Removes the Dalai Lama From Its Ads in Hong Kong
1998-04-17.abstract: Apple Computer, self-styled rebel of American computer industry, removes images of Dalai Lama from an advertising campaign in Asia for fear of offending China; Apple has used Tibet's exiled spiritual leader in United States, where he appears as one of series of revolutionary figures from politics, academia, culture and business; but he will be conspicuously absent from Apple's ads in Hong Kong; photo (M)

1998-04-18.title: In Death, Indignity for Despised Killer
1998-04-18.abstract: Thai officers poke and prod body of former Khmer Rouge leader Pol Pot, Sahook, Cambodia, taking fingerprints and other evidence to prove that man responsible for over million deaths is dead; examination falls short of full autopsy; foreign journalists view body; his widow, Mea Son, says he died in his sleep; Cambodian troops and Khmer Rouge defectors pursue last group of guerrilla holdouts, led by Ta Mok; map; photo (M)

1998-04-18.title: In the Battle for Pompeii, A Plan to Reverse Decay
1998-04-18.abstract: POMPEII—  Buried in a matter of hours under several meters of ash and pumice stone during a cataclysmic eruption of Vesuvius in 79 A.D., Pompeii is the most perfectly preserved city to come down to us from the ancient world. Last year almost 2 million people visited the site, well over half a million more than the Uffizi Gallery in Florence, making the antique ghost town the most popular single attraction in Italy.

1998-04-18.title: Israel Redefines Its Dream, Finding Wealth in High Tech
1998-04-18.abstract: High-tech revolution has transformed Israel, founded 50 years ago as socialist experiment by defiant European Jews, into Mideast Silicon Valley; nation abounds with signs of prosperity as high-tech exports grow; current indicators point to incipient recession and tensions with Palestinians have battered tourism and could start scaring off investors; graphs; map; photo (L)

1998-04-18.title: Awash in U.S. Aid. Could Float Fine Without It.
1998-04-18.abstract: Israel's new-found economic success leads many Israelis and Americans to question size of United States aid package; Israel, largest beneficiary of US aid, has been getting $3 billion in American economic and military aid for last several years, even as overall foreign aid budget has fallen; has proposed phasing out economic aid and increasing military aid, for eventual overall cut of 20 percent; Congressional members who favor larger cuts fear offending American Jews or Israel's powerful lobby (M)

1998-04-18.title: Net for Rebel Leaders
1998-04-18.abstract: Since Pol Pot's death, the Clinton Administration has intensified its effort to bring the leaders of the Khmer Rouge to trial, asking experts to prepare indictments of several figures for gross rights abuses, American officials said.

1998-04-19.title: Where Opium Reigned, Burmese Claim Inroads
1998-04-19.abstract: Myanmar, formerly Burma, produced estimated 2,600 tons of opium in 1997, enough to make over 200 tons of heroin--at least 60 percent of world total; military junta, which has tolerated opium trafficking since it seized power in 1988, says that it wants to eradicate all opium within five years; some diplomats view eradication claim skeptically because land devoted to opium cultivation has doubled under junta's rule and country's mismanaged economy has grown to rely on laundered drug profits; map; photo (M)

1998-04-19.title: A Clean Bill For the Iraqis On A-Arms? Experts Upset
1998-04-19.abstract: International Atomic Energy Agency is moving close to declaring Iraq free of nuclear weapons, and some US experts are raising alarms and charging agency with complacency; they say Iraq's record of deception and its wealth of talented scientists point to both intention and ability to recreate atomic program quickly (M)

1998-04-19.title: A Burmese Tangle Over Opium Trade
1998-04-19.abstract: Myanmar, formerly Burma, provides at least 60 percent of the world's supply of heroin, but the military Government insists it is ready to crack down and eradicate all opium production within five years.

1998-04-19.title: At Cremation Of Pol Pot, No Tears Shed
1998-04-19.abstract: Body of Pol Pot, one of century's great mass killers, is cremated near Cambodian-Thai border; only dozen people witness cremation, including small group of photographers who are invited to record event (M)

1998-04-19.title: Talks Between 2 Koreas Collapse in Mutual Blame
1998-04-19.abstract: First direct talks in four years between North and South Korea collapse, with each side blaming other; talks began in Beijing, China, on April 12 (M)

1998-04-20.title: New Austrian Cardinal Apologizes for Predecessor's Sex Crimes
1998-04-20.abstract: Christoph Cardinal Schonborn, head of embattled Austrian Roman Catholic Church, apologizes for behavior of predecessor, Hans Hermann Cardinal Groer, who is accused of having molested young seminarians (M)

1998-04-20.title: Reporter's Notebook; First Lady Visits Real People in Chile
1998-04-20.abstract: Reporter's Notebook on some activities of Pres Clinton and Hillary Rodham Clinton during visit to Chile (M)

1998-04-20.title: Wang Dan, a Leader of '89 Protests, Arrives in Detroit : China Dissident Frail but Free
1998-04-20.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Wang Dan, a leader of the 1989 democracy movement in Beijing, arrived Sunday in Detroit, a day after being freed from a Chinese prison in the latest in a series of moves aimed at easing strains in U.S.-China relations.

1998-04-20.title: Settler Killed, Palestinian Wounded in Clash Over Land
1998-04-20.abstract: Small group of unarmed Palestinian shepherds clashes with three Jewish settlers in Hebron, and a settler is killed (M)

1998-04-20.title: Brussels Journal; The Philosopher With a Pie, and a Glint in the Eye
1998-04-20.abstract: Profile of Noel Godin, Belgian pie-thrower who feels he must 'entarte' people who are particularly full of self-importance and particularly lacking sense of humor; Microsoft chairman, Bill Gates, was target in February; photo (M)

1998-04-21.title: Albright to See Netanyahu And Arafat In London
1998-04-21.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat will travel to London on May 4 for separate meetings with Sec of State Madeleine K Albright aimed at reviving stalled peace talks; announcement is made by British Prime Min Tony Blair after meeting with Arafat in Gaza while on Middle East tour; US State Department says meetings will be held to determine whether American proposals on further Israeli withdrawals from West Bank and other issues can provide basis for breakthrough and lead to talks on permanent peace settlement; photo (M)

1998-04-21.title: Archbishop Trevor Huddleston, 84, Dies; Fought Apartheid From Its Earliest Days
1998-04-21.abstract: Archbishop Trevor Huddleston, a British Anglican who spent years fighting apartheid in South Africa and helped make its abuses widely known, dies at age 84; photo (L)

1998-04-21.title: U.S. AND BRITAIN RELOCATE A CACHE OF NUCLEAR FUEL
1998-04-21.abstract: US and Britain begin classified operation to remove highly enriched uranium and spent nuclear fuel from volatile Caucasus nation of Georgia, fearing it could fall into hands of Chechen gangs, Iran or other aspiring nuclear power; American military and civilian personnel are at research reactor in Tblisi, using large protective containers to pack 8.8 pounds of highly enriched uranium and 1.76 pounds of highly radioactive spent fuel, which will be flown by US Air Force to Britain to be reprocessed at Dounreay nuclear complex in Scotland; operation was undertaken two years after efforts to enlist help from Russians failed and after France rebuffed US request to accept material; British played role after Clinton Administration refused to move it to US because of potential challenges from American environmentalists; Georgia will be paid approximately $125,000 for material; map (M)

1998-04-21.title: Hannover Races to Get Its Futuristic Expo Ready for Opening in 2000
1998-04-21.abstract: HANNOVER—  The Great Exhibition of 1851 in London, of which the Hanoverian Queen Victoria and her husband and cousin Prince Albert were prime movers, started the craze for giant international expositions. Curiously, although numerous similar events have been held around the globe since, no German city has ever hosted one. But today's Hanoverians will finally get the chance when the capital of Lower Saxony stages Expo 2000, which opens June 1 of the millennial year.

1998-04-21.title: Europeans Drop Lawsuit Contesting Cuba Trade Act
1998-04-21.abstract: European Union drops legal challenge to Helms-Burton Act, under which US can impose penalties on companies that use American properties in Cuba that were seized by Castro Government three decades ago; decision is major step toward defusing dispute over sanctions that has riled trans-Atlantic relations for more than three years; both sides caution they are still far from final settlement of dispute, and that if talks break down, European Union could file new action with World Trade Organization (M)

1998-04-22.title: Sinn Fein Is Relaxing Demeanor And Image
1998-04-22.abstract: Sinn Fein, Irish Republican Army's political wing, is rapidly changing its public image; has become less aggressive in public, more tolerant of oposition, funnier and even better dressed now that it has attained make-or-break role in Northern Ireland peace effort (M)

1998-04-22.title: At Inquiry, French Officials Say They Tried in Rwanda
1998-04-22.abstract: Ex-Prime Min Edouard Balladur and parade of high-ranking former French officials tell unusual televised French parliamentary hearing on France's military role in Rwanda that they did all they could to stop French-trained ethnic Hutu forces in 1994 from slaughering ethnic Tutsi minority; killings took more than 500,000 lives; French troops were stationed in Rwanda during period as part of United Nations peacekeeping force (M)

1998-04-22.title: Seoul Won't Seek Japan Funds for War's Brothel Women
1998-04-22.abstract: South Korea says it will end effort to win Japanese compensation for South Korean women forced into Japanese military brothels in World War II; will pay each of 152 registered 'comfort women' $22,700, supplemented by $4,700 each from victims' rights groups; Pres Kim Dae Jung pledges to continue to seek apology from Japan; says Government will not interfere with continued demands by women and nongovernment organizations for Japanese compensation (M)

1998-04-22.title: BEHIND THE BATTLE FOR RUSSIAN POST, ECONOMIC CONTROL
1998-04-22.abstract: Internal battle over future of Russian Government bursts into open as fight for control of economic policy between small group of big business owners who have amassed huge fortunes and Government economic reformers; newspaper owned by Boris Berezovsky, Russia's richest and most ruthless tycoon, announces that pitched battle over Pres Boris Yeltsin's nomination of Sergei N Kiriyenko as Prime Minister can only be avoided if Yeltsin abandons market reformers trying to better regulate sale of state property; Berezovsky is believed to be acting with other leading tycoons and has forged key alliances; ex-Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin echoes his call that reformer Anatoly B Chubais be denied post as head of electrical power monopoly; photos (M)

1998-04-22.title: I.M.F. Loans to Rights Violators Are Attacked in Congress
1998-04-22.abstract: Asst Treas Sec Timothy F Geithner, urging House subcommittee to approve added $18 billion for International Monetary Fund, faces harsh questioning on United States support for IMF bailout of Indonesia in light of nation's human rights violations; he and Karin Lissakers, US representative to IMF, hold Clinton Administration uses its influence to spur human rights; human rights dispute is part of broader Congressional examination of IMF (M)

1998-04-23.title: Blair Defends His Decision To Accept Atom Material
1998-04-23.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair, faced with outcry from political opponents, environmentalists and Scottish interests, defends decision to store and reprocess nuclear material in Scotland from old Soviet reactor; confirms New York Times report that Britain has agreed to US request to accept uranium and spent nuclear fuel (M)

1998-04-23.title: Accuser of Top Generals Slain in Mexico
1998-04-23.abstract: Thomas Arturo Gonzalez Velazquez, lawyer who accused top generals and relatives of Pres Ernesto Zedillo of narcotics graft as defense tactic in corruption trial of Mexican Government's drug czar, Gen Jesus Gutierrez Rebollo, is slain Guadalajara; photo (M)

1998-04-23.title: Americans, Flouting U.N. Embargo, Organize Relief for Iraqis
1998-04-23.abstract: Americans are beginning to react against policy of continued sanctions against Iraq by organizing their own relief projects for Iraqi people; Americares, private relief organization based in New Canaan, Conn, says it will carry out first American airlift of over 75,000 pounds of medicines and hospital supplies to Baghdad, where nearly eight years of sanctions have crippled civilian medical system (M)

1998-04-23.title: French Said to Hurt Plan to Capture Karadzic
1998-04-23.abstract: Senior US and Western officials say planning for possible capture of Bosnian Serb leader Radovan Karadzic was set back late last summer after US officials discovered that French military officer, Maj Herve Gourmillon, had held secret meetings with Karadzic and French were covering it up (M)

1998-04-23.title: In Borneo's Fading Jungles, a Grim Tale of Wildlife
1998-04-23.abstract: BALIKPAPAN, Indonesia—  One by one, Gabriella Fredriksson tracked down the five little bears she had released into the forest.

1998-04-24.title: Leak Caused NATO to Drop Bosnia Effort On Fugitives
1998-04-24.abstract: Senior US officials say plans for capturing people indicted on war-crimes charges in Bosnia, including fugitive Bosnian Serb leader Radovan Karadzic, went awry last year after US military officers learned that French Army Maj Herve Gourmelon had been meeting secretly with Karadzic (M)

1998-04-24.title: Iraq Again Threatens to Halt Arms Inspections
1998-04-24.abstract: Pres Saddam Hussein makes his most direct threat to provoke another crisis over arms inspections unless economic embargo on Iraq is lifted by United Nations Security Council; Iraqi Cabinet issues statement saying US will 'pay dearly' for not heeding warning; Deputy Prime Min Tariq Aziz demands in letter to Security Council that sanctions be lifted 'immediately without any new restrictions or conditions' (M)

1998-04-24.title: G.O.P. Snubs White House On Billions For the I.M.F.
1998-04-24.abstract: Congressional Republican leaders drop Clinton Administration's request for $18 billion for International Monetary Fund from popular spending bill, delivering potentially fatal blow to one of Pres Clinton's top foreign policy goals (M)

1998-04-24.title: Blair Blocks Release of Rules for Parades in Northern Ireland
1998-04-24.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair blocks publication of Government restrictions on Northern Ireland's flashpoint summer parades, saying that he wants to avoid destabilizing province at 'volatile time' (M)

1998-04-24.title: Especially in India/Fair Color as a Cultural Virtue : Creams for a Lighter Skin Capture the Asian Market
1998-04-24.abstract: NEW DELHI—  When Abha Bhatnagar was growing up, her mother forbade her to go outdoors. She was not trying to keep her daughter out of trouble. "She was trying to keep my skin from getting dark," said Mrs. Bhatnagar, who disobeyed the order. Now she regrets that her olive skin is not lighter. But she believes there is a solution: fairness cream, which she recently started using on her face.

1998-04-25.title: Bungling in Sex Criminal's Escape Riles Belgians
1998-04-25.abstract: Belgians already outraged by despicable crimes of pedophile-rapist Marc Dutroux are infuriated even more after he carries out brief escape from prison; photos; citizens who feel humiliated and ridiculed by bungling poice and, by extention, inept Government ask how Dutroux, who was recaptured after four hours, could have been left without handcuffs in custody of only one policeman; demands grow for Government to resign (S)

1998-04-25.title: Iran Nearly Got A Missile Alloy From Russians
1998-04-25.abstract: Truck laden with 22 tons of stainless steel suitable for making missiles is intercepted by customs officials in Azerbaijan en route from Russia to Iran; map; Russian officials had failed to halt shipment despite warning from US intelligence, raising questions about promise to clamp down on such sales to Iran; special alloy can be used to make fuel tanks for Scud missiles (S)

1998-04-25.title: U.S. Eyes Curbs on Belgrade As Albanian Deaths Mount
1998-04-25.abstract: US will seek freeze on Yugoslav assets held overseas, in response to warnings that heavy fighting in Kosovo Province is on verge of spilling into Albania; map; State Departments has reports of 'myriad deaths' in Kosovo, where 90 percent of population is ethnic Albanian (S)

1998-04-25.title: U.S., in a Shift, Backs U.N. Move to Curb Illicit Trade in Guns
1998-04-25.abstract: Clinton Administration, in major move against international crime, is co-sponsoring UN resolution aimed at curbing illicit trafficking in firearms; resolution drafted by Britain and Canada is rewritten to appease US gun lobbyists, by clarifying that it does not call for action on possession of handguns or domestic sales; NRA still opposes any UN role in firearms issues (S)

1998-04-25.title: U.S.-Russia Impasse Is Said to Delay a U.N. Debate on Iraqi Arms Inspections
1998-04-25.abstract: Standoff between US and Russia apparently derails plans for UN Security Council debate on changing level of nuclear inspections in Iraq from high surveillane to long-term monitoring, whch would be small gesture to Iraq; US Amb Bill Richardson cites concerns reported by inspection chief Richard Butler (S)

1998-04-26.title: U.S. Accord With Panama on Troops Hits a Snag
1998-04-26.abstract: Agreement between Panama and US on drug interdiction center that would have permitted American troops to stay in Panama after US gives up control of Panama Canal on Dec 31, 1999 has unraveled four months after it was announced by Pres Ernesto Perez Balladares; Panama has demanded extensive changes in accord, and negotiators say they do not know if they will be able to produce replacement; photo (M)

1998-04-26.title: Through Trade and Aid, Iraqi Official Says, Embargo Is Eroding
1998-04-26.abstract: Foreign Min Mohammed Said al-Sahaf says economic embargo imposed against Iraq in 1990 is eroding and with it Baghdad's isolation; views increased trade and donations, including from Americans, as evidence of general distaste for indefinite sanctions because of their effect on Iraqi people (M)

1998-04-26.title: 11 NATIONS TAKING NEXT PIVOTAL STEP TOWARD THE EURO
1998-04-26.abstract: Leaders of 11 European countries will meet in Brussels on May 2 to approve final plans for introduction of single currency on Jan 1; despite some remaining feuding, they are expected to spell out exchange rates at which their individual currencies will be locked together; France and Germany, two most important nations, are still fighting over who will run new European central bank as well as about basic economic principles; most economists say euro's long-term prospects are better than they dared imagine few years ago; graphs (M)

1998-04-26.title: Folk Hero Strips Teflon From Thailand's Corrupt
1998-04-26.abstract: Lieut Gen Seri Temiyavej is head of Thailand's Central Investigation Bureau, equivalent of FBI; Seri claims that he has never taken bribe; he has become national folk hero and symbol of reform effort that could reshape Thailand's notoriously corrupt political system; photo (M)

1998-04-26.title: For Better or Worse, Israel Is 'Special' in U.S. Eyes
1998-04-26.abstract: Its image has been tarnished, its need for billions in aid questioned in Congress. But to most Americans, Israel remains a worthy recipient of support, and to almost half it is a ''special place,'' rather than just another country, the latest nationwide New York Times poll shows.    American attitudes toward Israel have dipped and risen over its 50 years with the roll of events, but never by much. This month, 57 percent of 1,395 people polled across the country said they had a generally favorable opinion of Israel.

1998-04-27.title: Kohl's Party Routed in East; Far Right Enters Assembly
1998-04-27.abstract: Germany Chancellor Helmut Kohl's Christian Democrats are trounced in election in Saxony-Anhalt, Germany's slowest-growing state; German People's Union wins almost 12 percent of vote, putting far-right group into eastern state assembly for first time; photos (M)

1998-04-27.title: A Day in Court, and Justice, Sometimes, for the Chinese
1998-04-27.abstract: Ordinary Chinese are increasingly suing powers that be, including their employers, state enterprises and police, acts of defiance against their broad and previously unchecked powers over people's lives; respond to far-reaching overhaul of legal system and rapid changes in economic and social order; number of cases resolved in favor of plaintiff is rising fast, despite laws that are sometimes unclear and unpredictable courts; photo (L)

1998-04-27.title: Polish Aid Fund Turns Profit, Posing a Problem
1998-04-27.abstract: Clinton Administration and champions of Poland battle over proceeds of sale of $240 million in investments made by Polish American Enterprise Fund with foreign aid money; Zbigniew Brzezinski, Polish-born former national security adviser, opposes plan by Treasury and Agency for International Development to return half of money to United States, interview; national security adviser Samuel R Berger favors return of money (M)

1998-04-27.title: Ex-General Leads in a Crucial Vote in Siberia, but Faces a Runoff
1998-04-27.abstract: Aleksandr I Lebed enhances his presidential hopes by winning place in runoff election for governor of Krasnoyarsk, vast Russian region; partial tally shows he leads pack, with 43 percent of vote; Valery M Zubov, Kremlin-backed incumbent, is second, with 36 percent (M)

1998-04-27.title: Oranjemund Journal; Find a Diamond in the Sand? Just Don't Pick It Up
1998-04-27.abstract: De Beers Corp maintains tight security in Sperrgebeiet, rich diamond bed along Namibia's southern coast; expects diamonds will be depleted in another 25 years and weighs moves to open ecologically fragile desert area to public; map; photo (M)

1998-04-28.title: Jews and Arabs, Painting a Mural Together, Find a Mosaic of Mistrust
1998-04-28.abstract: Article in series Israel at 50 discusses how peace-loving artists Adi Yekutieli, Israeli Jew, Ahmed Bweerat, Israeli Arab, and Suliman Mansour, Palestinian, produced traveling exhibit of canvas murals that is symbolic guide to living together peacefully; notes that process proved far more tortured than any of them imagined, and was microcosm of mistrust and betrayal that mirrored hostilities they had set out to overcome; photos (M)

1998-04-28.title: Chretien Finds Castro Willing to Deal, but Not on Rights
1998-04-28.abstract: Canadian Prime Min Jean Chretien meets with Cuban Pres Fidel Castro in Havana; Castro agrees to negotiate foreign investment protection agreement with Canada and agrees to pay $10 million to Canadian insurance company that lost its business in Cuba after 1959 revolution; Chretien fails to win any concessions from Castro on human rights; photo (M)

1998-04-28.title: Israel Mural Reveals The Fault Lines at 50
1998-04-28.abstract: An effort to have an Israeli Jew, an Israeli Arab and a Palestinian work together to produce a mural depicting a future without fear proved far more tortured than any of them imagined -- a microcosm of mistrust and betrayal that mirrored the hostilities they had set out to overcome.    On a personal level, in private, the men forged a bond and shared a deep mutual respect. But burdened by history and communal expectations, their mural became a battleground of symbols.

1998-04-28.title: Restrictions on Iraq Will Stay in Force, U.N. Council Rules
1998-04-28.abstract: Security Council decides to extend sanctions against Iraq, ignoring threats from Baghdad that it will disrupt future arms inspections if sanctions are not lifted; Bill Richardson, US representative, says United Nations weapons inspectors have found that Iraq still has long way to go in accounting for all of its weapons of mass destruction; Council, in gesture to Iraqis, who have recently allowed greater access to arms inspectors, does not limit future sanctions reviews to six-month intervals but reverts to system of permitting reviews every 60 days; US has vowed to veto any lifting of sanctions until Iraq has met number of requirements, including verifiable disarmament and return of prisoners and property seized in invasion of Kuwait (M)

1998-04-28.title: Manavi Journal; Georgia's Celestial Wine: Is the World Ready?
1998-04-28.abstract: Wine has been part of life in Georgian Republic for over 5,000 years, and for much of that time Manavi and other villages in Kakheti district have been wine heartland; nearly every family has known nothing but wine business for many generations; each village produces distinct variety, and it is all but impossible to find anyone who does not swear that his or her village makes very finest; map; photo (M)

1998-04-29.title: U.N. Experts Report Finding Mustard Gas in Iraqi Shells
1998-04-29.abstract: Richard Butler, United Nations' chief arms inspector for Iraq, says experts found active mustard gas in artillery shells, raising new questions about over 500 similar shells still unaccounted for in Iraq; shells were found in 1996, but not tested until March, in face of Iraqi opposition; Min Mohammed Said al-Sahaf again holds Iraq destroyed all barred weapons in 1991 (M)

1998-04-29.title: 'People Power' Unrest on Indonesian Campuses
1998-04-29.abstract: Democracy drive is growing on university campuses across Indonesia; students seem unsure whether movement will end with bloody crackdown or people's revolution, but are increasingly willing to confront police; are gaining support among middle class, which blames Pres Suharto for economic crisis, by denouncing corruption and rising prices; protests are eroding Suharto's aura of authority and omnipotence and creating new confusion about where nation is heading; map; photos (M)

1998-04-29.title: And Interwoven With Civil Life : An Army Forged by Wars
1998-04-29.abstract: PARIS—  No army's forces have been so frequently battle-tested as those of Israel. And no other people has lived so long so massively outnumbered by surrounding foes and so convinced of being annihilated if their enemies got the chance as have Israelis.

1998-04-29.title: Japanese Move to Broaden Military Links to the U.S.
1998-04-29.abstract: Japanese Government offers bill to broaden military ties with United States and let it offer logistical support to American forces in Japan in event of crisis; measure brings Japan step closer to playing larger security role in region; Sec State Madeleine K Albright and Min Keizo Obuchi sign related accord that underscores expanded support from Japan (M)

1998-04-29.title: After Sound and Fury in Russia, Cabinet Change Is Slight
1998-04-29.abstract: First round of appointments to Russia's new Cabinet are announced after Pres Boris N Yeltsin meets with Prime Min Sergei N Kiriyenko, and it reveals no surprises, no political concessions and hardly any change; seems to confirm view that Yeltsin's sudden dismissal of his Cabinet was targeted at Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin and Anatoly B Chubais; another victim, Interior Min Anatoly S Kulikov, is replaced by Sergei V Sepashin; Boris Y Nemtsov, economic reformer, will remain Deputy Prime Minister, Yevgeny M Primakov Foreign Minister and Mikhail M Zadornov Defense Minister (M)

1998-04-30.title: Italy Savors Victory in Euro Race, But Doubt About Future Lingers
1998-04-30.abstract: Italy will be among 11 countries that will formally join European Monetary Union on May 2; only year ago it was considered one of least likely countries to qualify for single currency plan's strict financial controls; Prime Min Romano Prodi's coalition Government, supported by rare consensus among labor unions, business and taxpayers, made heroic strides in cutting spending and raising revenue to bring down Italy's deficit to European standards; concern is that once in, Italy will relax and fail to sustain necessary and more painful fiscal discipline and structural reform; graph (M)

1998-04-30.title: Debate in Ireland Centers on Provision to Free I.R.A. Prisoners
1998-04-30.abstract: National debate is under way in Irish Republic over provision of new Northern Ireland peace agreement that would grant early release to Irish Republican Army prisoners convicted of terrorist acts, possibly including murder; agreement will be put to separate referendums in Ireland and Northern Ireland on May 22 (M)

1998-04-30.title: Bias Said to Hurt Independent Mexican Unions
1998-04-30.abstract: Mexican Labor Ministry, responding to US Labor Dept report that found favoritism toward unions aligned with Mexico's ruling party in labor elections, criticizes US for going beyond terms of North American Free Trade Agreement (M)

1998-04-30.title: Senate Rejects Bid to Create a NATO Unit to Resolve Conflicts
1998-04-30.abstract: Senate rejects attempt to create tribunal within NATO to help resolve conflicts that might affect alliance before they erupt into full-blown hostilities; votes 62 to 37 to reject proposed amendment to plan to add Poland, Hungary and Czech Republic to military alliance (M)

1998-04-30.title: U.S. and China Gain Rapport, Albright Says
1998-04-30.abstract: Sec of State Madeleine K Albright meets with Chinese Foreign Min Tang Jiaxuan in Beijing; says US and China are building 'constructive, strategic partnership'; Albright has begun two days of high-level meetings; Pres Clinton will go to China for summit meeting in June (S)
